keys.description: When true (default), tenancy::TenantProvisioned dispatches SeedDefaultAgeGroupsForTenantJob. Off = the tenant lands without a default catalog; callers must create groups before any Team can carry an age_group_id. Off is only safe in migration harnesses where a bespoke seed handles the invariant.
Bumps when `data/default-age-groups.json` changes materially. Downstream consumers (Team picker cache, notification templates) compare against the cached copy + refetch when the numbers diverge.

When true, SeedDefaultAgeGroupsForTenantJob is dispatched to a queue. When false, AgeGroupCatalogSeeder runs synchronously in the tenant-provisioning transaction. Sync is safer (the tenant is never briefly catalogless) but slower. Async is the default because tenant onboarding must feel instant.
Cron expression for ReconcileAgeGroupDefaultInvariantJob. Daily 03:30 UTC by default (staggered from region reconciler at 03:15).
When true, drift (a (tenant, org, sport_key) tuple with 0 or 2+ default groups) fires the alert per `config('monitoring.channels.compliance')`. Off = drift is only logged; ops must poll audits.
When true, the reconciler auto-heals drift (promote-oldest). Off by default — surface + acknowledge before healing. Turn on in production when ops has built confidence in the drift patterns.
Platform-wide minimum for min_age_inclusive. 3 is deliberate — real programmes rarely start younger + tighter bounds guard against typos. Support tickets can extend this per-tenant.
Platform-wide maximum for max_age_inclusive. 100 is a sanity ceiling.

When true (default from Wave 3+), bounds mutations refuse when any active Athlete would become ineligible. When false, bounds mutations succeed but fire an audit-critical AgeGroupBoundsChanged with the impacted-athlete count. Wave 3a: this check is a no-op regardless — Athlete + AthleteEnrollment don't exist yet.

How AgeGroupResolver::resolveFor(...) behaves when multiple candidate AgeGroups match (a real risk when tenants overlap bounds across sport_key values or gender_categories). `refuse` = throw AGE_GROUP_RESOLVE_INDETERMINATE (the safe default; forces the caller to disambiguate). `first_match` = return the first row by sort_order. `oldest_group` = return the row with the smallest min_age_inclusive.

When resolving for an Athlete + sport_key, prefer the sport-specific override (sport_key non-null) over the generic (sport_key null) fallback. Off = always use the generic group; sport-specific groups become editorial (advisory-only) rather than authoritative.

Days a soft-deleted AgeGroup stays in the archived state before PurgeArchivedAgeGroupsJob hard-deletes it. Default 1095 (3 years) — matches rolling youth-development cohort windows. Compliance floor: 730 days (2 years). Enterprise tenants may extend up to 2555 (7 years matching audit retention).

Days audit rows produced by this module survive after their source AgeGroup is hard-deleted. Default 2555 (7 years — minor-safeguarding retention floor). Read-only reference — actual retention is enforced by the audit module's retention policy.
TTL for `age:list:{tenant_id}` — cached active-group list used by the tenant admin UI + every Team edit form's age-group dropdown.
TTL for `age:catalog:{tenant_id}` — cached rollup by (organization, sport_key) tuple used by the picker widget.
TTL for `age:resolve:{tenant_id}:{dob}:{sport_key}` — cached resolver answers. Short because the same DOB + sport_key answers may shift as the calendar rolls (rolling_from_birthday cutoffs).
